svg.text: MARLIE I — LAFAYETTE AI FACTORY & COMMAND CENTER
POWER DISTRIBUTION SCHEMATIC | FUEL TO CHIP | 4-LAYER POWER HIERARCHY | 1,040 kW IT
Sheet P-001 | Design Intent | 2026-03-23 | NOT FOR CONSTRUCTION

g.text: Both G3520C parallel to common 480V AC bus

svg.text: 800V DC MAIN DISTRIBUTION BUS — NVIDIA DSX REFERENCE
(DIFFERENT from Trappeys — Grid is BACKUP here, not sell-back)
960 sq ft roof = ~55 First Solar TR1 panels
Made 30 miles away in New Iberia, LA
Henry Hub 40 miles — cheapest in USA
~41% load per unit (1.24 MW on 3 MW)
Cloudy day + gas down = grid bridges
KEY DIFFERENCE: MARLIE I vs TRAPPEYS/WILLOW GLEN
Trappeys/Willow Glen: Grid = SELL-BACK only (Layer 4 revenue)
MARLIE I: Grid = BACKUP power (Layer 4 safety net) — smaller facility, grid supports ops
1. Gas is BACKBONE (Layer 2) — always running, carries full facility load. Solar offsets but does not replace gas.
2. Grid (LUS + SLEMCO) is BACKUP (Layer 4) — different from Trappeys where grid is sell-back only.
3. MARLIE I is smaller (1,920 sq ft vs 112,500 sq ft) — grid backup makes sense at this scale.
4. N+1 generator redundancy: 2x G3520C for 1.24 MW load. Either unit offline = zero impact.
5. Solar DC-Direct: 300 kW rooftop feeds 800V bus at 97% efficiency. ~24% offset of total load.
6. BESS 600 kWh provides ride-through during transfer events + battery bridge for short outages.
